terms.label: Admissibility
terms.definition: The property of a proposed transition being allowed to proceed under the authority, policy, evidence, and context state present at commit time.

terms.label: Transition
terms.definition: A movement from one governed state to another that may carry consequence.

terms.label: Authority Class
terms.definition: A declared category of permission or standing that an actor, agent, service, repository, or governed component may hold for a proposed transition.

terms.label: Policy Reference
terms.definition: The specific rule, policy, standard, or governance artifact used to evaluate a proposed transition.

terms.label: Evidence Posture
terms.definition: The state of the evidence used to evaluate a proposed transition, such as present, missing, stale, incomplete, conflicting, sufficient, or insufficient.

terms.label: Review Posture
terms.definition: The state of human, automated, delegated, or quorum-based review for a proposed transition.

terms.label: Drift
terms.definition: A relevant change in identity, authority, policy, evidence, review, context, or system state that may affect whether a proposed transition remains admissible.

terms.label: Commit-Time Authority
terms.definition: The authority basis that exists at the moment a transition result binds.

terms.label: Commit-Time Validity
terms.definition: The condition that a transition remains valid at the moment its result is allowed to bind.

terms.label: Receipt-Bound Execution
terms.definition: Execution connected to a durable record of the authority, policy, evidence, context, and decision posture present at commit time.

terms.label: Governance Boundary
terms.definition: The declared limit of what a system claims to govern, observe, authorize, enforce, or prove.

terms.label: Reconstructability
terms.definition: The ability to recover enough information after an event to understand what happened and why a decision appeared to have standing.

terms.label: Transition Table
terms.definition: A StegVerse formalism surface for classifying proposed state changes and their governance posture.

terms.label: ALLOW Decision
terms.definition: A transition result indicating that the proposed transition has standing to bind under the declared authority, policy, evidence, review, and commit-time context.

terms.label: DENY Decision
terms.definition: A transition result indicating that the proposed transition does not have standing to bind consequence under the declared commit-time conditions.

terms.label: ESCALATE Decision
terms.definition: A transition result indicating that the current evaluator cannot allow or deny the transition without higher, different, or additional review.

terms.label: REFUSE Decision
terms.definition: A transition result indicating that the evaluator declines to process the request as framed because it is outside declared scope, violates a boundary, or lacks a valid transition basis.

terms.label: Drift Denial
terms.definition: A denial caused by relevant state change between prior review and commit time, such that the transition no longer has standing to bind.

terms.label: Proposal Lifecycle
terms.definition: The path by which a proposed wiki or governance change is submitted, reviewed, decided, recorded, and made reconstructable.

terms.label: Page Decision Record
terms.definition: A record that explains why a wiki page or page change was accepted, denied, escalated, refused, superseded, deferred, or deprecated.

terms.label: Terminology Convergence
terms.definition: The wiki process of mapping StegVerse terms to identical, overlapping, adjacent, broader, narrower, or contradictory terms from other domains.

terms.label: Terminology Relationship
terms.definition: A declared relationship between a StegVerse term and a term from another domain, such as equivalent, overlapping, adjacent, broader, narrower, or contradictory.

terms.label: Equivalent Term
terms.definition: An external term that describes the same underlying concept as the StegVerse term for the page's purpose.

terms.label: Overlapping Term
terms.definition: An external term that partially describes the same concept but does not carry the full StegVerse meaning.

terms.label: Adjacent Term
terms.definition: An external term from the same conceptual neighborhood that answers a different governance question than the StegVerse term.

terms.label: Replay Link
terms.definition: A pointer from a page, proposal, receipt, or decision record to the replayable artifact or command that can re-check the declared result.

terms.label: Reconstruction Link
terms.definition: A pointer from a page, proposal, receipt, or decision record to the evidence needed to reconstruct why a decision appeared to have standing.
